'''''UFC 300: Pereira vs. Hill''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on April 13, 2024, at the [[T-Mobile Arena]] in [[Paradise, Nevada]], part of the [[Las Vegas Valley]], United States.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/12/ufc-300-fight-card-date-location-news|title=UFC 300 officially announced for Las Vegas|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2023-12-15|access-date=2023-12-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2023/12/15/24003602/ufc-confirms-event-dates-locations-including-ufc-299-ufc-300|title=UFC confirms event dates, locations including UFC 299, UFC 300|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Steven Marrocco|date=2023-12-15|access-date=2023-12-16}}</ref>

==Background==
===Main card: three title fights and a title eliminator===
[[File:T-Mobile Arena (31534778122).jpg|thumb|left|250px|After hosting [[UFC 200]] in 2016, [[T-Mobile Arena (Las Vegas)|T-Mobile Arena]] hosted another milestone event, which was its 28th UFC event overall.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.thescore.com/mma/news/2794903|title=UFC 300 to take place April 13 in Las Vegas|publisher=thescore.com|author=Nick Baldwin|date=2023-12-15|access-date=2023-12-22}}</ref>]]

A [[UFC Light Heavyweight Championship]] bout between current champion [[Alex Pereira]] (also former [[List of UFC champions#Middleweight Championship|UFC Middleweight Champion]] and former [[Glory (kickboxing)#Glory Middleweight Championship|Glory Middleweight]] and [[Glory (kickboxing)#Glory Light Heavyweight Championship|Light Heavyweight Champion]]) and former champion [[Jamahal Hill]] headlined the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/02/ufc-300-main-event-fight-card-alex-pereira-vs-jamahal-hill|title=UFC 300 main event announced: Alex Pereira vs. Jamahal Hill for light heavyweight title|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2024-02-18|access-date=2024-02-18}}</ref> The fight was verbally agreed for [[UFC 301]] three weeks later, but the promotion opted to book it at this card.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/02/ufc-300-alex-pereira-signs-news-contract-says-fight-bumped-up-brazil|title=Alex Pereira signs new 8-fight contract before UFC 300, says fight was bumped up from UFC 301|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2024-02-21|access-date=2024-02-21}}</ref> Pereira won the vacant title at [[UFC 295]] against former champion [[Jiří Procházka]] after Hill was forced to vacate it in July 2023 due to a ruptured [[Achilles tendon]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2023/7/14/23791259/jamahal-hill-relinquishes-ufc-light-heavyweight-title-suffering-ruptured-achilles-tendon-basketball|title=Jamahal Hill relinquishes UFC title after suffering ruptured Achilles in basketball game|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2023-07-14|access-date=2024-02-18}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bleacherreport.com/articles/10096935-alex-pereira-defeats-jiri-prochazka-by-ko-to-win-light-heavyweight-title-at-ufc-295|title=Alex Pereira defeats Jiří Procházka by KO to win light heavyweight title at UFC 295|publisher=bleacherreport.com|author=Alex Ballentine|date=2023-11-12|access-date=2024-02-18}}</ref>

A [[UFC Women's Strawweight Championship]] bout between current two-time champion [[Zhang Weili]] and [[Yan Xiaonan]] took place in the co-main event. It marked the first time two Chinese-born athletes met for a title in the organization.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/01/ufc-300-news-zhang-weili-vs-yan-xiaonan-all-china-strawweight-title-fight-april-13|title=UFC 300 adds Zhang Weili vs. Yan Xiaonan in all-China strawweight title fight|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2024-01-10|access-date=2024-01-10}}</ref>

Former interim [[UFC Lightweight Championship|UFC Lightweight Champion]] [[Justin Gaethje]] (also former [[WSOF Lightweight Championship|WSOF Lightweight Champion]]) put his symbolic "BMF" (''baddest [[motherfucker]]'') title on the line against former [[UFC Featherweight Championship|UFC Featherweight Champion]] [[Max Holloway]] in a lightweight bout.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/platform/amp/2024/1/16/24040941/ufc-300-justin-gaethje-defends-bmf-belt-vs-max-holloway-jim-miller-gets-bobby-green|title=UFC 300: Justin Gaethje defends BMF belt vs. Max Holloway, Jim Miller gets Bobby Green|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Steven Marrocco|date=2024-01-16|access-date=2024-01-16}}</ref> Gaethje won the symbolic title at [[UFC 291]] defeating fellow former interim champion [[Dustin Poirier]] by second-round knockout in what was their second meeting.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/38098344/justin-gaethje-knocks-dustin-poirier-wins-bmf-title-ufc-291|title=Justin Gaethje knocks out Dustin Poirier, wins BMF title at UFC 291|work=espn.com|author=Brett Okamoto|date=2023-07-30|access-date=2024-01-16}}</ref> The inaugural [[UFC Heavyweight Championship|UFC Heavyweight Champion]] and [[UFC Hall of Fame]]r [[Mark Coleman]] (also [[Pride Grand Prix 2000 Finals|PRIDE 2000 Open Weight Grand Prix champion]]) delivered the belt to the winner.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/4/11/24127967/ufc-hofer-mark-coleman-to-put-bmf-belt-on-justin-gaethje-vs-max-holloway-winner-at-ufc-300|title=UFC HOFer Mark Coleman to put 'BMF' belt on Justin Gaethje vs. Max Holloway winner at UFC 300|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2023-04-11|access-date=2024-04-13}}</ref>

Former [[UFC Lightweight Championship|UFC Lightweight Champion]] [[Charles Oliveira]] and [[Arman Tsarukyan]] met in a lightweight title eliminator bout.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/platform/amp/2024/1/8/24029557/charles-oliveira-vs-arman-tsarukyan-announced-for-ufc-300-winner-to-face-islam-makhachev|title=Charles Oliveira vs. Arman Tsarukyan announced for UFC 300, winner to face Islam Makhachev|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2024-01-07|access-date=2024-01-07}}{{Dead link|date=January 2026 |bot=InternetArchiveBot }}</ref>

The main card was opened by a middleweight bout between [[NCAA Division I Wrestling Championships#1999–2024|three-time NCAA Division I National Wrestling Champion]] and undefeated prospect [[Bo Nickal]] and [[Cody Brundage]].<ref name="Firstbouts"/>

===Under-card===
Former [[UFC Light Heavyweight Championship|UFC Light Heavyweight Champion]] [[Jiří Procházka]] (also the inaugural [[Rizin Fighting Federation#Light Heavyweight Championship|Rizin Light Heavyweight Champion]]) and [[Aleksandar Rakić]] met in a light heavyweight contest as the featured bout of the preliminary card.<ref name="Firstbouts">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/12/ufc-300-dana-white-announces-first-ufc-300-bookings-jiri-prochazka-aleksander-rakic-aljamain-sterling-calvin-kattar-bo-nickal|title=Dana White announces first UFC 300 bookings with Prochazka-Rakic, Sterling-Kattar, Bo Nickal|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2023-12-21|access-date=2023-12-21}}</ref>

Former [[UFC Bantamweight Championship|UFC Bantamweight Champion]] [[Aljamain Sterling]] made his promotional debut at featherweight against [[Calvin Kattar]].<ref name="Firstbouts"/>

[[Judo at the 2012 Summer Olympics – Women's 78 kg|2012]] and [[Judo at the 2016 Summer Olympics – Women's 78 kg|2016 Olympic gold medalist in judo]] (also two-time [[Professional Fighters League|PFL]] women's lightweight tournament champion) [[Kayla Harrison]] made her promotional debut, as well as her bantamweight debut, against former [[UFC Women's Bantamweight Championship|UFC Women's Bantamweight Champion]] (also [[International Boxing Hall of Fame]]r) [[Holly Holm]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/1/23/24048764/kayla-harrison-signs-with-ufc-set-to-debut-at-bantamweight-against-holly-holm-at-ufc-300|title=Kayla Harrison signs with UFC, set to debut at bantamweight against Holly Holm at UFC 300|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2024-01-23|access-date=2024-01-23}}</ref>

A featherweight bout between [[Sodiq Yusuff]] and [[Diego Lopes (fighter)|Diego Lopes]] took place at the event.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/01/ufc-300-diego-lopes-vs-sodiq-yusuff-booked|title=Diego Lopes vs. Sodiq Yusuff joins stacked UFC 300 lineup|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Farah Hannoun|date=2024-01-24|access-date=2024-01-24}}</ref>

[[Renato Moicano]] and [[Jalin Turner]] met in a lightweight bout, which was the final addition to the card.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/03/ufc-300-news-renato-moicano-vs-jalin-turner-completes-lineup-historic-event-las-vegas|title=Renato Moicano vs. Jalin Turner completes lineup for historic UFC 300 event|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2024-03-09|access-date=2024-03-10}}</ref>

Former [[UFC Women's Strawweight Championship|UFC Women's Strawweight Champion]] [[Jéssica Andrade]] and [[Marina Rodriguez]] met in a women's strawweight bout.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/1/26/24051869/jessica-andrade-vs-marina-rodriguez-set-for-ufc-300|title=Jessica Andrade vs. Marina Rodriguez set for UFC 300|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Mike Heck|date=2024-01-26|access-date=2024-01-26}}</ref>

[[Jim Miller (fighter)|Jim Miller]] and [[Bobby Green]] met in a lightweight bout at the event. Miller became the only fighter to have competed at all three milestone events for the organization, previously competing at [[UFC 100]] and [[UFC 200]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/01/ufc-300-fight-card-jim-miller-vs-bobby-green|title=Jim Miller gets UFC 300 wish, fights fellow vet Bobby Green at historic event|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2024-01-16|access-date=2024-01-16}}</ref> The duo was previously booked thrice, but canceled all three times due to Green withdrawals: first at [[UFC 172]] in April 2014, due to an elbow injury;<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.mmatorch.com/artman2/publish/UFC_2/article_21134.shtml|title=With Bobby Green injured, UFC 172 shakes up lightweight bouts with Miller-Medeiros and Ellenberger-Rocha now booked|publisher=mmatorch.com|author=Jamie Penick|date=2014-04-20|access-date=2021-02-01}}</ref> the second booking at [[UFC 258]] in February 2021, when he collapsed after the weigh-ins and was deemed unfit to compete;<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/02/ufc-258-bobby-green-collapses-after-weigh-ins|title=Bobby Green collapses after UFC 258 weigh-ins, pulled from Jim Miller fight|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Simon Samano|date=2021-02-12|access-date=2021-02-12}}</ref> and the third time at [[UFC 276]] in July 2022, when Green withdrew for unknown reasons.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2022/06/ufc-276-donald-cerrone-vs-jim-miller-bobby-green-injury-mma-matchup-booking|title=Donald Cerrone replaces Bobby Green, fights Jim Miller in rematch at UFC 276|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Danny Segura and Mike Bohn|date=2022-06-23|access-date=2022-06-23}}</ref>

A bantamweight bout between former [[UFC Bantamweight Championship|UFC Bantamweight Champion]] [[Cody Garbrandt]] and former two-time [[UFC Flyweight Championship|UFC Flyweight Champion]] [[Deiveson Figueiredo]] was chosen to open the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmanews.com/news/deiveson-figueiredo-booked-bantamweight-return-ufc-300|title=Deiveson Figueiredo booked for bantamweight return at UFC 300 against a former champion|publisher=mmanews.com|author=Curtis Calhoun|date=2024-01-11|access-date=2024-01-11}}</ref> The pairing was previously scheduled to meet at [[UFC 255]] in November 2020 for the flyweight title but Garbrandt withdrew due to a torn bicep.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://globoesporte.globo.com/combate/noticia/cody-garbrandt-deixa-luta-e-deiveson-figueiredo-vai-enfrentar-alex-perez-pelo-cinturao.ghtml|title=Cody Garbrandt pulls out and Deiveson Figueiredo will face Alex Perez for the belt|publisher=globoesporte.globo.com|author=Marcelo Barone and Raphael Marinho|date=2020-10-02|access-date=2024-01-11}} {{in lang|pt-br}}</ref>

===Additional information===
The event originally featured $50,000 fight night bonuses, which were raised to $300,000 2 days before the event, marking the highest amount ever paid by the promotion for such bonuses.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/ufc/ufc-300-press-conference-dana-white-bonuses-b2527663.html|title=Dana White agrees to $300,000 bonuses for UFC 300 fighters|publisher=independent.co.uk|author=Alex Pattle|date=2023-04-12|access-date=2024-04-13}}</ref>

During the event's broadcast, it was announced that a [[UFC Middleweight Championship]] bout that took place in August 2010 at [[UFC 117]] between then-champion [[Anderson Silva]] (also a [[UFC Hall of Fame]]r himself) and challenger [[Chael Sonnen]] (also a former [[UFC Light Heavyweight Championship]] challenger) was the next "fight wing" [[UFC Hall of Fame]] inductee. Anderson defended the title via fifth-round submission in what was considered one of the greatest comebacks in MMA history.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/04/anderson-silva-vs-chael-sonnen-1-ufc-117-joins-2024-hall-of-fame-class-fight-wing|title=Anderson Silva vs. Chael Sonnen 1 joins 2024 UFC Hall of Fame class|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Matthew Wells|date=2024-04-14|access-date=2024-04-14}}</ref>

==Bonus awards==
The following fighters received $300,000 bonuses.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmamania.com/2024/4/14/24129437/official-post-fight-bonus-winners-ufc-300-results-espn-ppv-bmf-mma-pereira-hill-holloway-prochazka|title=Bonuses! Max Holloway banks insane $600K for 'BMF' knockout - UFC 300|publisher=mmamania.com|author=Dan Hiergesell|date=2024-04-13|access-date=2024-04-13}}</ref>
*'''Fight of the Night: Max Holloway vs. Justin Gaethje'''
*'''Performance of the Night: Max Holloway''' and '''Jiří Procházka'''

==Aftermath==
The Nevada State Athletic Commission (NSAC) has withheld the purses of [[Arman Tsarukyan]] and [[Diego Lopes (fighter)|Diego Lopes]] due to their behavior at the event, where Arman Tsarukyan allegedly struck a fan on his way to the octagon, and Diego Lopes for jumping out of the cage.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmauncensored.com/2024/04/26/nsac-ufc-300-purses-tsarukyan-lopes/|title=NSAC to hold hearings on withholding UFC 300 purses for Arman Tsarukyan and Diego Lopes|author=|date=2024-04-26|publisher=mmauncensored}}</ref> Both fighters met with the NSAC on April 30, 2024, to discuss the incidents.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmamania.com/2024/4/26/24141307/nac-to-discuss-withholding-of-purse-for-ufc-300-fighters-arman-tsarukyan-and-diego-lopes|title=NSAC to discuss 'withholding of purse' for UFC 300 fighters Arman Tsarukyan and Diego Lopes|author=|date=2024-04-16|publisher=mmamania}}</ref> The NSAC decided to withhold 20% of Tsarukyan's fight purse ($31,600 of $158,000) and $5,000 of Lopes' $100,000 payout.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-04-30 |title=Arman Tsarukyan & Diego Lopes Face UFC 300 Repercussions; Fighter Fined for Infamous Bite |url=https://www.si.com/fannation/mma/ufc/ufc-300-tsarukyan-lopes-fined |access-date=2024-05-13 |website=MMA Knockout |language=en-US}}</ref>

This event won the [[2024 in UFC#2024 UFC Honors awards|UFC Honors ''2024 Event of the Year'']] award as voted by the fans.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.homeoffight.com/news/ufc/ufc-honors-2024-event-recap|title=UFC Honors 2024 Event Recap|publisher=homeoffight.com|author=Mike Jones|date=2025-01-13|access-date=2025-01-15|archive-date=2025-02-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250207104833/https://www.homeoffight.com/news/ufc/ufc-honors-2024-event-recap|url-status=dead}}</ref>
